title: Catch Semantics, Collection Identity, Randomness, and Deterministic Evidence · Go Build Room
summary: A regional field-research team incrementally builds deterministic owned Pokemon collection transition from original fixed PokéAPI fixtures while retaining prior Go, HTTP, Git, quality-gate, accessibility, security, testing, cancellation, and recovery requirements. Planned artifact: Build a complete first version of deterministic owned Pokemon collection transition while every earlier competency remains active. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal…
objectives: Define whether catch is guaranteed, probabilistic, skill-based, or a collection action and state duplicate and failure semantics.
If probability is used, inject a narrow source, record seed or draw evidence, bound values, and keep policy separate from randomness.
Key owned entries by resolved stable ID while retaining display name and capture evidence without raw-input duplicates.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: Pokedex product engineering and Go Commit to the mission model · Workshop Pokedex Go Catch Product…
steps.instruction:
workshop case — A workshop Go team handles case 9100 during an accessible regional field-guide session. Build deterministic Go evidence to reconcile deterministic catch decisions with stable identity and unchanged duplicate state; no user-visible meaning may rely on color or cursor motion; then inject one cache, waiter, goroutine, cancellation, or queue schedule and retain ownership evidence. Browser Go uses pure bounded models and original fixtures only. Real PokéAPI traffic, DNS, sockets, TLS, host files, terminals, signals, race and leak claims, load, cross-platform packaging, accessibility review, and production behavior require explicit controlled transfer gates.

What must be established before implementation?

Evidence target: Pokedex product engineering and Go Commit to the mission model · Workshop Pokedex Go Catch Product….
